behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler↔signature-verifier↔queue handoff: must prove a bad signature both returns 401 and suppresses enqueue. No real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process wiring catches the real bug. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires "rejected with a 401" and "not forwarded," not real HTTP/network timing.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with real signature-check module and a spy/fake queue adapter; send mismatched raw body+header and assert HTTP 401 plus zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler and persistent queue write path). A unit test of one function is too low; but real OS/network is not required if using in-process app + real queue persistence adapter (e.g., local DB/file). Rejected higher level: behavior does not require real network/subprocess, only write-before-ack order.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process HTTP invocation against the real handler wired to the real persistent queue module; instrument response completion and queue commit point, asserting commit occurs before 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at handoff boundary between request handler, queueing, and async processor trigger. Can be reproduced with in-process modules by making processor intentionally slow and verifying response is not blocked. Rejected higher level: behavior text is about not waiting for processing, not real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue + a controllable slow processor promise; send valid event and assert response completes before processor resolves.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is runtime concurrency + wall-clock latency under load; in-process fakes/timers can hide real event-loop and HTTP-server contention. Requires real server behavior and real time measurement. Rejected lower levels: wording explicitly requires "within 3 seconds wall-clock" under concurrent slow work.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real service process (or full runtime stack), create concurrent backlog of slow downstream jobs, send webhook requests over real HTTP, and assert end-to-end response latency stays under 3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: This is dedupe state handoff across handler and persistent queue/idempotency store; unit-only logic risks missing contract/state bugs. No real OS/network/LLM dependency is implied. Rejected higher level: behavior text is local idempotency ("ID already received"), not external infra behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the real handler+queue/idempotency modules and assert exactly one persisted queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is adapter/contract mapping from queue-full signal to HTTP status+header. Needs module interaction, but does not require real OS/network. Rejected higher level using behavior text: phrase "when the queue reports it is full" allows deterministic in-process queue-full condition injection.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue module configured to return/throw a full-capacity condition; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: Target is queue component correctness with real persistence path (module + storage), not just mocked full signals. This is beyond pure unit logic but still local infra (DB/filesystem) rather than OS/network/subprocess necessity. Rejected higher level: behavior says capacity enforcement in the queue itself, not HTTP/network runtime effects.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue backend with a small configured capacity, enqueue up to limit, then assert next enqueue is rejected and persisted size does not exceed configured cap.
